source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A bush has 10 twigs. Each twig has exactly 5 leaves or exactly 2 leaves and a flower. Which of the following numbers could be the total number of leaves on the bush?
<image1>
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: None of the numbers from (A) to (D)

choice_map.E: None of the numbers from (A) to (D)

image_dependency: Question is fully specified in text and does not reference any diagram, chart, or visual attribute
An image asset exists in the registry, but no textual evidence suggests it is needed to solve the problem
multi_step: Solving requires modeling two twig types across 10 twigs and relating twig counts to total leaves
A typical solution combines conditions into a numeric form (e.g. total leaves determined by counts of 5-leaf twigs vs 2-leaf-plus-flower twigs)
Then the candidate totals must be checked against the derived constraint, so it is more than direct lookup
verifiability: Multiple-choice options are provided, making final-answer checking straightforward
answer_text matches one listed choice: 'None of the numbers from (A) to (D)'
The problem has a discrete numeric constraint structure, so solution steps and final choice can be rule-checked
registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present
